பாடக்குறிப்புகள்.விவரங்கள்: The story 'The Mistaken Plate' revolves around two merchants who sold things made of brass and tin. They travelled across rivers and mountains, dividing the streets to avoid disturbing each other. One merchant was honest, while the other was greedy and always tried to pay as little as possible. In an old house, a poor grandmother (Ammama) and her granddaughter (Kanna) lived. They had a single plate left from their wealthy past, kept in memory of the grandfather. The greedy merchant was called to value the plate. He scratched it with a needle, realized it was pure gold, but lied, saying it was not even worth one rupee, and threw it on the ground to buy it cheaper later. Later, the honest merchant passed by. He respectfully examined the plate, truthfully informed them it was made of gold, and admitted he was not rich enough to buy it. When the grandmother offered to trade it, he gave them all his money, wares, donkey, and cart, keeping only eight rupees to pay the ferry boatman. When the greedy merchant returned offering ten rupees, he was shocked to learn the honest merchant had already bought it by paying a huge price. The story highlights that honesty brings true reward, while greed leads to loss.

Let Us Read Aloud (Supplementary Passage): This section contains a story about a poor but honest farmer who lived with his wife and seven children. He found a bag of gold in his field. Though his wife asked him to keep some, he believed 'honesty is the best policy' and returned the bag to its rightful rich owner, who thanked him. Let Us Write (Dialogue Writing): Introduces dialogue structure. Contains conversations between Miss Meena's students (Ravi and Glory) discussing a story project, and a conversation between Rathi and a Shopkeeper about buying a ten-rupee pen.

பாடக்குறிப்புகள்.தலைப்பு: 3. Points to Remember and One-Mark Areas
பாடக்குறிப்புகள்.விவரங்கள்: Metals sold by merchants: Brass and Tin. True metal of the plate: Gold. Ferry charge given to the boatman: 8 rupees. Greedy merchant's first fake valuation: Not even 1 rupee. Greedy merchant's second offer: 10 rupees. Tool used to scratch the plate: A needle. Family members in the old house: Grandmother (Ammama) and Granddaughter (Kanna). Number of farmer's children: Seven. Price of the pen Rathi bought: 10 rupees.

A. Choose the correct answer: 1. The merchants sold things made of brass. 2. The grandmother had kept the plate in memory of her husband. 3. In real the plate was made of gold. 4. The merchant paid eight rupees to the boatman. 5. Secondly the greedy merchant is ready to offer ten rupees. B. Whose words are these?: 1. 'Utensils made of brass and tin for sale!' - Both Merchants. 2. 'I am not rich enough to buy this plate. This is a gold plate.' - Good Merchant. 3. 'We do not have money to buy anything.' - Grandmother (Ammama). C. I Can Do (Vocabulary & Grammar): Synonyms: Foot hills - low hill at base; Kingdom - country. Animal Sounds: Monkey - chatter; Elephant - trumpet; Rooster - crow. Rhyming Words: say - day; go - grow; all - fall. Articles: the Himalayas; the Vaigai; an old man; the Sun.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What kind of items did the two merchants sell?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: Things made of gold and silver
Things made of wood and glass
Things made of brass and tin
Things made of iron and steel
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: Things made of brass and tin
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The story clearly states that the merchants sold things made of brass and tin.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Why did the two merchants divide the streets of the town?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: They did not want to disturb each other while selling
They were ordered by the king
They wanted to race each other
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: They did not want to disturb each other while selling
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: They divided the streets so they wouldn't compete or disturb each other in the same area.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Who lived in the old house?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: An old woman (grandmother) and her granddaughter lived in the old house in poverty.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: In whose memory did the grandmother keep the old plate?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The text mentions, 'The grandmother had kept the plate in memory of her husband.'

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What affectionate name did the grandmother use to call her granddaughter?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The grandmother addresses her granddaughter by saying, 'Kanna, we do not have money...'

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What did the greedy merchant use to scratch the old plate?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The greedy man took the plate and scratched it with a needle to test the metal.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What did the greedy merchant realize after scratching the plate?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: It was a useless tin plate
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: He found out the true value of the plate, which was that it was made of gold.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What value did the greedy merchant verbally claim the plate had?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: To cheat them, he said, 'What is it worth? Not even one rupee.'

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What did the greedy merchant do immediately after lying about the plate's value?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: He threw the plate on the ground and walked away
He kept it in his bag secretly
He gave them a brass vessel
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: He threw the plate on the ground and walked away
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: He threw it down and left, planning to return later and buy it for a very cheap price.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Why did the granddaughter suggest showing the plate to the second merchant?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: He looked nice and the first merchant was angry
She knew the plate was gold
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: He looked nice and the first merchant was angry
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: She said, 'Grandma, that merchant was angry. This merchant looks nice. Maybe we will get something.'

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What was the honest merchant's reaction upon realizing the plate was gold?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: He said he was not rich enough to buy it
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: He said he was not rich enough to buy it
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: He honestly told them, 'I am not rich enough to buy this plate. This is a gold plate.'

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What did the honest merchant give to the grandmother in exchange for the plate?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: All his money, his donkey, his cart, and his wares
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: All his money, his donkey, his cart, and his wares
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: He gave everything he had to trade fairly for the valuable gold plate.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: How much money did the honest merchant keep for himself?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: He only kept eight rupees for the ferry to go home.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: To whom did the honest merchant pay the eight rupees?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: He paid the eight rupees to the boatman to take him across the river.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: When the greedy merchant returned to the old house, how much did he offer for the plate?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: He called the girl and said, 'I've changed my mind. I will give you ten rupees for it.'

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Identify the speaker: 'Ammama, please buy something for me.'
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The granddaughter said this when she heard the merchant calling in the street.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Identify the speaker: 'I am not rich enough to buy this plate. This is a gold plate.'
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The honest (second) merchant said this when he checked the plate.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Identify the speaker: 'Are you sure? This is gold? The other merchant threw this on the ground...'
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The grandmother said this in shock when the honest merchant told her the truth.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: In the 'Let Us Read Aloud' section, what kind of person was the farmer?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The passage begins with, 'There lived a poor but an honest farmer in a village.'

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: How many children did the honest farmer have?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The passage states he supported himself, his wife, and seven children.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What did the farmer find while walking in his field?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: One day while he was walking in his field, he found a bag of gold.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What did the farmer's wife suggest he do with the found item?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: Return it to the owner immediately
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: His wife told him to keep some of the gold, but he refused.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What is the main idea of the story about the farmer?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The farmer proved that 'honesty is the best policy' by returning the gold bag.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: In the dialogue writing section, what project did Miss Meena ask the class to do?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The text says, 'Miss Meena has asked the class to write a story as a project.'

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: In the shop dialogue, what does Rathi want to buy?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: Rathi visits the shop and asks the shopkeeper, 'I need a pen.'

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What is the meaning of the word 'Foot hills'?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: According to the 'I Can Do' section, Foot hills means 'low hill at base'.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What is the meaning of the word 'Kingdom'?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: According to the vocabulary section, Kingdom means 'country'.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Identify the correct sound made by a Monkey.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: Monkeys make a 'chatter' sound.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Identify the correct sound made by an Elephant.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: Elephants make a 'trumpet' sound.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Identify the correct sound made by a Rooster.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: Roosters make a 'crow' sound.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Find the correct rhyming word for 'say'.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: Say rhymes with day.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Find the correct rhyming word for 'go'.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: Go rhymes with grow.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Find the correct rhyming word for 'all'.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: All rhymes with fall.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Fill in the blank with the correct article: ____ Himalayas.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: We use 'The' before names of mountain ranges: The Himalayas.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Fill in the blank with the correct article: ____ Vaigai.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: We use 'The' before names of rivers: The Vaigai.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Fill in the blank with the correct article: ____ old man.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: 'Old' starts with a vowel sound, so we use 'An'.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Fill in the blank with the correct article: ____ Sun.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: We use 'The' for unique objects in nature: The Sun.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Match the characters with their actions: 1. Greedy Merchant A. Paid 8 rupees, 2. Good Merchant B. Scratched with a needle, 3. Boatman C. Received all wares.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: Greedy merchant scratched the plate. Good merchant gave all wares. Boatman was paid 8 rupees.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Assertion (A): The greedy merchant threw the golden plate on the ground. Reason (R): He thought the plate was actually worthless and made of cheap tin.
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: A is true, but R is false
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: He knew it was gold. He threw it to pretend it was worthless so he could buy it cheap later. So, Reason (R) is false.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Analogy: Monkey : Chatter :: Rooster : ?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: Just as a monkey chatters, a rooster crows.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Life Situation: You find a classmate's expensive pen on the floor. What is the most honest thing to do based on the lessons learned?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: Keep it for yourself since you found it.
Sell it to another student for money.
Give it to the teacher or the rightful owner.
Leave it on the floor and walk away.
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: Give it to the teacher or the rightful owner.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: Like the honest farmer and the good merchant, honesty dictates returning lost or valuable items to their rightful owners.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Assertion (A): The honest merchant gave all his wares, cart, and money to the grandmother. Reason (R): He wanted to cheat her by giving useless things instead of cash.
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: Both A and R are true
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
Both A and R are false
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: A is true, but R is false
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: He gave everything because he knew the plate was very valuable and he wanted to be as fair as possible, not to cheat her.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What was the greedy merchant's ultimate punishment for his dishonesty?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: He lost the golden plate forever
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: He lost the golden plate forever
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: Because he lied and waited to get it cheaper, the good merchant bought it, leaving the greedy merchant with nothing.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Match the prices with the correct context: 1. Ten Rupees A. Ferry charge, 2. Eight Rupees B. Fake valuation, 3. Zero Rupees C. Second offer.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: 10 rupees was the second offer. 8 rupees was the ferry charge. Zero (not even 1 rupee) was the fake valuation.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Analogy: Good Merchant : Truthful :: Greedy Merchant : ?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The good merchant told the truth, while the greedy merchant lied and deceived.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Choose the correct sequence of events: A) The greedy merchant scratches the plate. B) The honest merchant pays his wares. C) The greedy merchant offers 10 rupees. D) The granddaughter asks to buy something.
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: First the girl asks to buy. Greedy man checks and scratches it. Good man buys it. Greedy man returns with 10 rupees.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: What statement summarizes the farmer's belief when he found the bag of gold?
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The text explicitly states: 'He told his wife that honesty is the best policy.'

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Who was always greedy as he tried to pay as less as possible?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: The second merchant mentioned in the intro
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: The second merchant mentioned in the intro
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The story's introduction says, 'The second merchant was always greedy as he tried to pay as less as possible.'

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Life Situation: A shopkeeper mistakenly gives you more change than you deserve. Based on the lesson, what should you do?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: Return the extra money to the shopkeeper
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: Return the extra money to the shopkeeper
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: Honesty requires returning what does not rightfully belong to you.

தேர்வுகள்.கேள்வி: Assertion (A): The grandmother and granddaughter lived in poverty. Reason (R): They only had one old plate left as a memory of their past wealth.
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: Both A and R are true, and R supports A
Both A and R are true, but R does not support A
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
தேர்வுகள்.விடை: Both A and R are true, and R supports A
தேர்வுகள்.விளக்கம்: The fact that they only had one plate left from their wealthy past clearly illustrates their current state of poverty.
